title: Nephrodium achalense
intro: Nephrodium achalense, also known as Chalaleño, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium achalense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nephrodium achalense is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and cold.
